model_narrative: We plan motor production across a sequence of seasons. In each season we decide how many motors to produce, which work shifts to open and how many employees to staff on each, how many employees to hire or fire, how much inventory to carry forward, and whether to take a lease option for the horizon. Producing motors consumes materials and labor along a piecewise-linear curve that links employees to motor output, carrying inventory and renting space costs money, and adjusting the workforce up or down through hiring and firing also costs money. Future costs are discounted to present value. The objective is to minimize the total discounted cost over the whole horizon.

sets.name: q
sets.doc: the planning seasons in chronological order; the first member is the opening season and each later member immediately follows the one before it

sets.name: s
sets.doc: the work shifts that can be operated within a season

sets.name: l
sets.doc: the breakpoints of a piecewise-linear production curve, indexed in increasing order; each breakpoint pairs a labor level with the motor output achievable at that labor level

sets.name: i
sets.doc: auxiliary production intervals; not referenced by any constraint in this model

params.name: d
params.doc: the number of motors demanded in each season, in motors

params.name: lc
params.doc: the leasing cost charged in a season if the lease option is taken, in dollars

params.name: ei
params.doc: the initial employment level associated with each season, in employees; informational and not referenced by any constraint

params.name: delt
params.doc: the discount factor applied to a season's costs to bring them to present value; a dimensionless multiplier

params.name: mc
params.doc: the material cost incurred per motor produced, in dollars per motor

params.name: sr
params.doc: the space rental cost charged per motor of inventory held, in dollars per motor

params.name: hc
params.doc: the cost of hiring one employee, in dollars per employee

params.name: fc
params.doc: the cost of firing one employee, in dollars per employee

params.name: invmax
params.doc: the maximum inventory level, equal to the total demand summed over all seasons, in motors

params.name: pr_motor
params.doc: the piecewise-linear production curve evaluated at each breakpoint; the 'labor' row gives the number of employees consumed at that breakpoint and the 'motor' row gives the number of motors produced at that breakpoint; the first index takes the literal values 'labor' and 'motor', the second index runs over the breakpoints

params.name: sc
params.doc: the shift operating cost coefficients; the 'fixed' row gives the fixed cost charged when a shift is opened and the 'labor' row gives the cost charged per employee working that shift; the first index takes the literal values 'fixed' and 'labor', the second index runs over the shifts

vars.name: cost
vars.doc: the total present-value cost over the whole horizon, in thousands of dollars

vars.name: dpc
vars.doc: the direct production cost in each season, in thousands of dollars

vars.name: isc
vars.doc: the inventory-related cost in each season, in thousands of dollars

vars.name: wfc
vars.doc: the workforce adjustment cost in each season, in thousands of dollars

vars.name: src
vars.doc: the space rental cost in each season, in dollars

vars.name: p
vars.doc: the number of motors produced in each season

vars.name: ss
vars.doc: the weight placed on each breakpoint of the piecewise-linear production curve for a given season and shift; the weights for a shift sum to whether that shift is opened

vars.name: ssb
vars.doc: the binary selector accompanying the piecewise-linear breakpoint weights for a given season and shift; not referenced by any constraint in this model

vars.name: inv
vars.doc: the number of motors held in inventory at the end of each season

vars.name: lease
vars.doc: the lease option indicator; equals 1 if the lease is taken and 0 otherwise

vars.name: e
vars.doc: the total number of employees on staff in each season

vars.name: se
vars.doc: the number of employees working a given shift in each season

vars.name: shift
vars.doc: the shift-open indicator; equals 1 if the shift is operated in that season and 0 otherwise

vars.name: h
vars.doc: the number of employees hired in each season

vars.name: f
vars.doc: the number of employees fired in each season

description: The total discounted cost gathers the costs incurred in every season and brings each season's costs to present value. For each season add together its direct production cost, its inventory cost, and its workforce cost, weight that seasonal total by the season's discount factor, and set the total cost equal to the sum of these discounted seasonal totals over all seasons.

description: The direct production cost of a season covers materials and the operating cost of its shifts, expressed in thousands of dollars. For each season, charge the material cost for every motor produced that season, and for each shift add the fixed cost incurred when the shift is opened together with the per-employee cost for everyone staffed on that shift. Sum these amounts and scale the result to thousands of dollars, and set the season's direct production cost equal to it.

description: Motor output in a season is read off the piecewise-linear production curve through the weights placed on its breakpoints. For each season, the number of motors produced equals the sum over both shifts and all breakpoints of the breakpoint weight multiplied by the motor output associated with that breakpoint.

description: The breakpoint weights for a shift behave as a convex combination tied to whether the shift is opened. For each season and each shift, the breakpoint weights for that shift must sum to the shift-open indicator, so they sum to one when the shift is operated and to zero when it is not.

description: Inventory carries forward from one season to the next while absorbing production and meeting demand. For each season, the inventory held at the end of the season equals the inventory carried in from the immediately preceding season plus the motors produced that season minus the motors demanded that season. In the very first season there is no preceding season, so nothing carries in.
expected_pyomo:
def invb_rule(m, q):
    qlist = list(m.q)
    idx = qlist.index(q)
    prev = m.inv[qlist[idx-1]] if idx > 0 else 0
    return m.inv[q] == prev + m.p[q] - m.d[q]
model.invb = Constraint(model.q, rule=invb_rule)

model_narrative: We plan motor production across a sequence of seasons. Future costs are discounted to present value. The objective is to minimize the total discounted cost over the whole horizon.

vars.doc: the number of employees working a given shift in each season

vars.name: shift
vars.doc: the shift-open indicator; equals 1 if the shift is operated in that season and 0 otherwise

vars.name: h
vars.doc: the number of employees hired in each season

vars.name: f
vars.doc: the number of employees fired in each season

description: The inventory cost of a season combines any leasing charge with the space rental cost, expressed in thousands of dollars. For each season, take the leasing cost charged that season only if the lease option is taken, add the season's space rental cost, scale the result to thousands of dollars, and set the season's inventory cost equal to it.

model_narrative: We plan motor production across a sequence of seasons. In each season we decide how many motors to produce, which work shifts to open and how many employees to staff on each, how many employees to hire or fire, how much inventory to carry forward, and whether to take a lease option for the horizon. Producing motors consumes materials and labor along a piecewise-linear curve that links employees to motor output, carrying inventory and renting space costs money, and adjusting the workforce up or down through hiring and firing also costs money. Future costs are discounted to present value. The objective is to minimize the total discounted cost over the whole horizon.

sets.name: q
sets.doc: the planning seasons in chronological order; the first member is the opening season and each later member immediately follows the one before it

sets.name: s
sets.doc: the work shifts that can be operated within a season

sets.name: l
sets.doc: the breakpoints of a piecewise-linear production curve, indexed in increasing order; each breakpoint pairs a labor level with the motor output achievable at that labor level

sets.name: i
sets.doc: auxiliary production intervals; not referenced by any constraint in this model

params.name: d
params.doc: the number of motors demanded in each season, in motors

params.name: lc
params.doc: the leasing cost charged in a season if the lease option is taken, in dollars

params.name: ei
params.doc: the initial employment level associated with each season, in employees; informational and not referenced by any constraint

params.name: delt
params.doc: the discount factor applied to a season's costs to bring them to present value; a dimensionless multiplier

params.name: mc
params.doc: the material cost incurred per motor produced, in dollars per motor

params.name: sr
params.doc: the space rental cost charged per motor of inventory held, in dollars per motor

params.name: hc
params.doc: the cost of hiring one employee, in dollars per employee

params.name: fc
params.doc: the cost of firing one employee, in dollars per employee

params.name: invmax
params.doc: the maximum inventory level, equal to the total demand summed over all seasons, in motors

params.name: pr_motor
params.doc: the piecewise-linear production curve evaluated at each breakpoint; the 'labor' row gives the number of employees consumed at that breakpoint and the 'motor' row gives the number of motors produced at that breakpoint; the first index takes the literal values 'labor' and 'motor', the second index runs over the breakpoints

params.name: sc
params.doc: the shift operating cost coefficients; the 'fixed' row gives the fixed cost charged when a shift is opened and the 'labor' row gives the cost charged per employee working that shift; the first index takes the literal values 'fixed' and 'labor', the second index runs over the shifts

description: Space rental cost in a season must cover the cost of renting space for the inventory held beyond what the lease already provides. For each season, the space rental cost must be at least the per-motor rental rate applied to the inventory held that season after deducting the maximum inventory whenever the lease option is taken.

description: The workforce cost of a season reflects the cost of hiring and firing, expressed in thousands of dollars. For each season, charge the hiring cost for every employee hired and the firing cost for every employee fired that season, scale the total to thousands of dollars, and set the season's workforce cost equal to it.

description: Total employment in a season is the staff spread across its shifts. For each season, the total number of employees on staff equals the sum of the employees working each shift that season.

description: Employment carries forward from one season to the next and changes only through hiring and firing. For each season, the total employment equals the employment carried in from the immediately preceding season plus the employees hired that season minus the employees fired that season. In the very first season there is no preceding season, so nothing carries in.
expected_pyomo:
def eb2_rule(m, q):
    qlist = list(m.q)
    idx = qlist.index(q)
    prev_e = m.e[qlist[idx-1]] if idx > 0 else 0
    return m.e[q] == prev_e + m.h[q] - m.f[q]
model.eb2 = Constraint(model.q, rule=eb2_rule)
